key_points: The poem describes Mrs. Jones’s unusual garden
Everything in the garden is extremely small and delicate
The garden includes tiny features like a crazy path, rockery, lily pond and sundial
The narrator visits the garden and observes its strange features
The poem ends humorously when the narrator realizes he was standing on the tiny flower
examples: The crazy path represents a tiny winding pathway in the garden
The lily pond is a miniature water feature
The rockery is made of small stones arranged decoratively
The sundial shows time in the small garden
The flower under the narrator’s feet shows how tiny the garden is
literary_elements: Humor → The poem uses exaggeration to make the garden amusing
Imagery → Descriptions help readers imagine the tiny garden
Hyperbole → The smallness of the garden objects is exaggerated
exam_tips: Remember that Mrs. Jones owns the unusual miniature garden
Focus on the humorous twist at the end of the poem
Understand how exaggeration creates humor in the poem
Know the message: even the smallest things can be interesting
